articles.title: 7 MassDOT employees on leave after TV news investigation into overtime
articles.description:
“If anyone is found to have been intentionally misreporting their time or misusing taxpayer dollars, they will be held accountable.” 

The post 7 MassDOT employees on leave after TV news investigation into overtime appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Seven MassDOT employees are on leave following an overtime investigation by local TV news.
MassDOT officials promise accountability for any intentional misreporting of time or misuse of funds.
The investigation raises concerns about taxpayer dollars and transparency in state employment practices.

articles.title: Jarren Duran’s 2-run, opposite field homer rallies Red Sox to a 4-3 win over the Royals
articles.description:
Willson Contreras had three of the Red Sox' 11 hits, including his second triple in four years.

The post Jarren Duran’s 2-run, opposite field homer rallies Red Sox to a 4-3 win over the Royals appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jarren Duran hit a 2-run homer, leading the Red Sox to a 4-3 victory over the Royals.
Willson Contreras contributed three hits, including his second triple in four years.
The win boosts the Red Sox's standing, energizing local fans and the community.

articles.title: Theo Epstein shares his thoughts on state of 2026 Red Sox
articles.description:
"Having to figure out the offense and fixing it midstream is important."

summary: Theo Epstein emphasizes the need to improve the Red Sox offense by 2026.

Boston residents can expect strategic changes to enhance local baseball experience.

articles.title: Person who attacked Harvard student from behind snuck into MIT dorm shortly after, police say
articles.description:
The person was “acting suspiciously" and matched the description of a person who snuck into Harvard's Lowell House.

The post Person who attacked Harvard student from behind snuck into MIT dorm shortly after, police say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Suspect attacked a Harvard student from behind on May 20, 2026.
The attacker matched the description and entered an MIT dorm shortly after.
Increased security concerns for students at both Harvard and MIT campuses.

articles.title: Police: Woman, 85, found dead in R.I. hotel after son dies by suicide
articles.description:
The woman's death is being investigated as a homicide, police said.

summary: Police found an 85-year-old woman dead in a Rhode Island hotel room.
Her death is being investigated as a homicide following her son's suicide.
This incident raises concerns about mental health and safety for Boston families.

articles.title: Massachusetts State Police Academy will pause recruit training to fix flaws
articles.description:
State police officials say they need time to carry out recommendations made by an outside team after a trainee died in a police academy boxing match.

The post Massachusetts State Police Academy will pause recruit training to fix flaws appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts State Police Academy pauses recruit training to address safety flaws.
Recommendations from an outside team follow a trainee's death during a boxing match.
Local residents may see delayed police recruitments and increased scrutiny on academy practices.

articles.title: If Bruins are looking to make a deal, the Ducks are an appealing trade partner
articles.description:

articles.title: Shark season is here. One just popped up in a Rhode Island pond.
articles.description:
Several Rhode Island residents spotted a porbeagle shark, which later beached and died, near Narragansett.

The post Shark season is here. One just popped up in a Rhode Island pond. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Residents spotted a porbeagle shark near Narragansett, Rhode Island.
The shark beached and died, raising concerns for local beachgoers.
Shark season may affect swimming safety along Boston's coastal areas.

articles.title: Families continue to fight BPS exam school admissions policy after judge rules no discrimination against white, Asian students
articles.description:
The Boston Parent Coalition argues in a new appeal that zip codes and tiers “both operate as a proxy for race" in school admissions.

summary: Boston Parent Coalition appeals judge's ruling on exam school admissions policy.
Families argue zip codes and tiers act as racial proxies in admissions.
Continued legal battles may affect future admissions for Boston students.

articles.title: Patriots receivers well aware of the A.J. Brown trade rumors
articles.description:

summary: Patriots receivers acknowledge A.J. Brown trade rumors affecting team dynamics.
